ogTitle: North Plympton vs North West Bend: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Plympton and North West Bend.
heading: North Plympton vs North West Bend — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Plympton, South Australia and North West Bend, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Plympton has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to North West Bend (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Plympton or North West Bend safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Plympton has a flood risk score of 22/100, while North West Bend scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Plympton scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. North West Bend scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Plympton or North West Bend?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Plympton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
